challenges.title: Data Storage
challenges.description: Create at least two HTML elements that use data-* attributes. Include at least three different data attributes across the elements (e.g., data-id, data-name, data-value). Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code:
import re
data_attrs = re.findall(r'data-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*=', html.lower())
assert len(data_attrs) >= 3, 'Should contain at least 3 data-* attributes'
attr_names = set(re.findall(r'(data-[a-z][a-z0-9-]*)=', html.lower()))
assert len(attr_names) >= 3, 'Should use at least 3 different data-* attribute names'
hints: Data attributes start with data- followed by a name: data-id="123"
You can create any custom data attribute like data-name, data-value, etc.

challenges.title: Interactive Data
challenges.description: Create an unordered list (<ul>) with at least 3 list items (<li>). Each list item should have both a data-category and a data-price attribute. Assign your HTML to the variable `html`.
challenges.test_code:
assert '<ul' in html.lower(), 'Should contain a <ul> element'
assert html.lower().count('<li') >= 3, 'Should contain at least 3 <li> elements'
assert html.lower().count('data-category=') >= 3, 'Each list item should have a data-category attribute'
assert html.lower().count('data-price=') >= 3, 'Each list item should have a data-price attribute'
hints: Each <li> needs both data-category and data-price attributes
